title: Belling the Cat
lines: The Mice held a council to discuss how they might protect themselves from the Cat.
"The Cat is our greatest enemy," said the oldest Mouse.
"We must devise some plan to protect ourselves."
"I have a plan," said one young Mouse.
"If we could tie a bell to the Cat's neck, we would hear her coming and have time to hide."
All the other Mice agreed that this was a fine plan.
"But who," asked the oldest Mouse, "will undertake to tie the bell to the Cat's neck?"
The young Mouse who had suggested the plan fell silent, and so did all the others.
"It is one thing to suggest a plan," said the old Mouse, "and quite another to carry it out."
moral: It is easy to propose impossible remedies.
